channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The Little Probe That Could: Why Voyager 1 Matters, and Why NASA Just Switched Part of It Off
item.description: This week, NASA announced it had shut down one of that spacecraft's remaining science instruments — not because the mission has failed, but to keep it alive a little longer.
item.content:encoded:
This week, NASA announced it had shut down one of that spacecraft's remaining science instruments — not because the mission has failed, but to keep it alive a little longer.

(Image credit: NASA)
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx

item.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles toward sea
item.description: South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff says the launches happened on Sunday morning from the North's eastern Sinpo area.
item.content:encoded:
South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff says the launches happened on Sunday morning from the North's eastern Sinpo area.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: US Coast Guard spots overturned vessel near Saipan during search for missing ship
item.description: The Mariana is a 145-foot dry cargo vessel registered in the U.S. It suffered engine failure Wednesday as a massive typhoon bore down on Saipan and nearby islands.
item.content:encoded:
The Mariana is a 145-foot dry cargo vessel registered in the U.S. It suffered engine failure Wednesday as a massive typhoon bore down on Saipan and nearby islands.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Life jacket worn by a passenger who survived the Titanic auctioned off for over $900,000
item.description: A life jacket worn by a passenger on the RMS Titanic has sold at auction for 670,000 pounds, which is more than $900,000.
item.content:encoded:
A life jacket worn by a passenger on the RMS Titanic has sold at auction for 670,000 pounds, which is more than $900,000.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A mass shooting in Ukraine's capital leaves 6 dead before police shot and killed the gunman
item.description: A gunman has killed six people and injured at least 14 in a mass shooting in Ukraine's capital before he was shot and killed by police.
item.content:encoded:
A gunman has killed six people and injured at least 14 in a mass shooting in Ukraine's capital before he was shot and killed by police.

(Image credit: Dan Bashakov)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump signs order fast tracking review of psychedelics for mental health disorders
item.description: President endorses psilocybin and ibogaine: "Can I have some, please?"
item.content:encoded:
President endorses psilocybin and ibogaine: "Can I have some, please?"

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Mandalit del Barco

item.title: Photos: How overfishing in Southeast Asia is an ecological and human crisis
item.description: A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contested.
item.content:encoded:
A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contested.

(Image credit: Nicole Tung)
item.dc:creator: Nicole Tung

item.title: Opinion: Remembering Kevin Klose, former NPR president and broadcasting icon
item.description: We remember Kevin Klose, former NPR president, who helped secure financial stability for the network while supporting and encouraging its journalism.
item.content:encoded:
We remember Kevin Klose, former NPR president, who helped secure financial stability for the network while supporting and encouraging its journalism.

(Image credit: Jay Paul)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Tired of waiting for your EV to charge up? One Chinese company has a novel solution
item.description: Chinese car company NIO is putting up EV battery swapping stations all around the world. NPR took a ride in one car for the experience.
item.content:encoded:
Chinese car company NIO is putting up EV battery swapping stations all around the world. NPR took a ride in one car for the experience.

(Image credit: Tang Ke/VCG via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: Born in south Lebanon, displaced to Beirut, two grandmothers reflect on Israeli invasions
item.description: They grew up amid olive groves in southern Lebanon. The son of one married the other's daughter. Now they're living temporarily in a vacant building in central Beirut, displaced many times.
item.content:encoded:
They grew up amid olive groves in southern Lebanon. The son of one married the other's daughter. Now they're living temporarily in a vacant building in central Beirut, displaced many times.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer
